title: Flatten
description:
Given a 2-D tensor $a \in \mathbb{R}^{i\times j}$, return its row-major flattening into a length-$(i\cdot j)$ vector, i.e. output$[r\cdot j + c] = a[r,c]$. `view`/`reshape` are banned — build the flattening purely from index arithmetic.

**Shapes**: `a`:[i,j] -> output:[i*j].
starter_code:
import torch

def flatten(a, i, j):
    # a: [i, j], return length i*j row-major vector (no view/reshape allowed)
    # TODO
    pass
hints: For flat position `k` in `arange(i*j)`, its row is `k // j` and its column is `k % j`.
Index `a` with those two derived index tensors directly (advanced indexing) instead of calling `view`/`reshape` — 1 line, broadcasting only.

solutions.name: Row/col index arithmetic
solutions.explanation: Row-major flattening maps flat index $k$ to row $\lfloor k/j\rfloor$ and column $k \bmod j$. Building those two index vectors with `arange(i*j)` and integer div/mod, then advanced-indexing `a` with them, reproduces `a.flatten()` without ever calling `view` or `reshape`.
